clauses.label: Confidentiality Scope
clauses.text: Recipient shall hold in strict confidence all proprietary information disclosed by the Disclosing Party, including all technical, business, and financial information, whether or not marked confidential.
clauses.risk_note: Broad scope favors disclosing party. Standard language.

clauses.label: Term & Duration
clauses.text: The obligations of confidentiality shall survive for five (5) years following termination of this Agreement.
clauses.risk_note: 5-year survival is standard for commercial NDAs.

clauses.label: Return of Materials
clauses.text: Upon termination, Recipient shall return or destroy all Confidential Materials within thirty (30) days.
clauses.risk_note: 30-day window is standard. Consider adding a certification requirement.

clauses.label: Governing Law
clauses.text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California.
clauses.risk_note: California is an acceptable jurisdiction for commercial agreements.

clauses.label: Remedies
clauses.text: The Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief in addition to any other remedies available at law or in equity.
clauses.risk_note: Standard injunctive relief provision.

clauses.label: Confidentiality Scope
clauses.text: Recipient shall hold in confidence only information that is specifically marked 'Confidential' at the time of disclosure.
clauses.risk_note: Narrowly scoped — only marked materials protected. Oral disclosures unprotected.

clauses.label: Term & Duration
clauses.text: The obligations of confidentiality shall survive for three (3) years following termination of this Agreement.
clauses.risk_note: 3-year survival is below the 5-year market standard.

clauses.label: Return of Materials
clauses.text: Upon termination, Recipient shall return or destroy all Confidential Materials within sixty (60) days.
clauses.risk_note: 60-day window is longer than standard 30 days.

clauses.label: Governing Law
clauses.text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of California.
clauses.risk_note: California jurisdiction acceptable.

clauses.label: Indemnification
clauses.text: Recipient shall indemnify and hold harmless the Disclosing Party against any and all losses arising from unauthorized disclosure, without limitation of liability.
clauses.risk_note: Unlimited indemnification with no liability cap is highly unusual and risky.

clauses.label: Scope of Services
clauses.text: Provider shall develop, deliver, and maintain the software and technology services described in mutually executed Statements of Work.
clauses.risk_note: Clear scope delegation to SOWs. Standard approach.

clauses.label: Intellectual Property
clauses.text: All work product created by Provider for Client shall be works-made-for-hire. Provider retains ownership of its pre-existing tools and methodologies ('Background IP').
clauses.risk_note: Works-for-hire favorable for client but review Background IP carve-out scope.

clauses.label: Data Security
clauses.text: Provider shall maintain industry-standard security controls and notify Client of any confirmed security incident within forty-eight (48) hours.
clauses.risk_note: 48-hour incident notification is standard.

clauses.label: Limitation of Liability
clauses.text: Each party's aggregate liability shall not exceed the greater of fees paid in twelve (12) months or $500,000, except for gross negligence, willful misconduct, or IP infringement.
clauses.risk_note: Balanced cap with appropriate carve-outs.

clauses.label: Dispute Resolution
clauses.text: Disputes shall be submitted to binding arbitration under JAMS rules in San Francisco, California.
clauses.risk_note: JAMS arbitration is standard.

title: Vendor SaaS Master Services Agreement v2

clauses.label: Indemnification
clauses.text: Client shall indemnify Provider against all third-party claims arising from Client's use of the Services, including data uploaded by Client, without cap or exclusion.
clauses.risk_note: One-sided uncapped client indemnity strongly favors Provider.

clauses.label: Limitation of Liability
clauses.text: Provider's total liability shall not exceed fees paid in the three (3) months preceding the claim. Provider shall have no liability for indirect, consequential, or punitive damages.
clauses.risk_note: Three-month fee cap is below market; no reciprocal cap on Client.

clauses.label: Intellectual Property
clauses.text: Provider retains all rights in the platform, models, and aggregated usage data. Client receives a non-exclusive license during the term only.
clauses.risk_note: No ownership of custom configurations or integrations.

clauses.label: Data Security
clauses.text: Provider shall use commercially reasonable efforts to protect Client data. Provider may use anonymized data for product improvement.
clauses.risk_note: 'Commercially reasonable' is vague; training use on client data is implied.

clauses.label: Termination
clauses.text: Provider may terminate for convenience on thirty (30) days notice. Client may terminate only for uncured material breach.
clauses.risk_note: Asymmetric termination rights favor Provider.

clauses.label: Confidentiality Scope
clauses.text: Each party shall protect the other's Confidential Information disclosed in connection with a potential transaction, whether oral, written, or electronic.
clauses.risk_note: Balanced mutual scope covering unmarked oral disclosures with reasonable care standard.

clauses.label: Term & Duration
clauses.text: Confidentiality obligations survive for five (5) years; trade secrets survive indefinitely.
clauses.risk_note: Market-standard survival with trade secret carve-out.

clauses.label: Return of Materials
clauses.text: Upon request, each party shall return or certify destruction of Confidential Materials within thirty (30) days.
clauses.risk_note: Mutual certification requirement is best practice.

clauses.label: Governing Law
clauses.text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York, without regard to conflicts principles.
clauses.risk_note: New York is common for enterprise M&A NDAs.

clauses.label: Limitation of Liability
clauses.text: Neither party shall be liable for indirect or consequential damages arising from breach; direct damages capped at $1,000,000.
clauses.risk_note: Mutual liability cap appropriate for exploratory NDAs.

clauses.label: Severance Payment
clauses.text: Employer shall pay six (6) months base salary in a lump sum within thirty (30) days of the Separation Date, subject to signing this Agreement.
clauses.risk_note: Verify COBRA and tax gross-up treatment; six months may be below market for executives.

clauses.label: Non-Compete
clauses.text: Employee shall not engage in competing business activities within a fifty (50) mile radius for twelve (12) months following separation.
clauses.risk_note: Enforceability varies by state; California generally voids non-competes post-2024.

clauses.label: IP Assignment
clauses.text: Employee assigns to Employer all inventions conceived during employment relating to Employer's business, including post-separation improvements to prior work.
clauses.risk_note: Post-separation IP sweep is broad; confirm prior inventions schedule.

clauses.label: Release of Claims
clauses.text: Employee releases all claims against Employer through the Separation Date, including unknown claims under ADEA, with seven (7) day revocation period.
clauses.risk_note: ADEA compliance requires specific language and timing; verify OWBPA checklist.

clauses.label: Confidentiality
clauses.text: Employee shall maintain confidentiality of Employer trade secrets and customer lists indefinitely.
clauses.risk_note: Standard post-employment confidentiality.

clauses.label: Purchase Price Adjustment
clauses.text: Purchase price increased from $12,400,000 to $12,750,000 reflecting updated appraisal and tenant improvement credits.
clauses.risk_note: Verify appraisal contingency and credit allocation against LOI.

clauses.label: Due Diligence Extension
clauses.text: Buyer's inspection period extended by fifteen (15) days to complete Phase I ESA and roof structural review.
clauses.risk_note: Extension is reasonable for environmental and structural items.

clauses.label: Governing Law
clauses.text: This Amendment and the underlying Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Texas.
clauses.risk_note: Texas law consistent with property location.

clauses.label: Dispute Resolution
clauses.text: Disputes shall be resolved by litigation in state or federal courts located in Travis County, Texas.
clauses.risk_note: No arbitration; buyer loses forum flexibility if seller is out-of-state.

clauses.label: As-Is Condition
clauses.text: Property sold strictly AS-IS with seller disclaiming all warranties except clear title and known material defects disclosed in Schedule B.
clauses.risk_note: Broad as-is language shifts repair risk to buyer post-closing.
